title: Crash-Candle Reversion v1 - OOS Protocol (PRE-REGISTRATION, protocol only)
head_ref_note: HEAD 923e786 (Crypto-D11 IS_WATCH) or descendant; factory tree clean; nothing staged; no active OOS run.
1_purpose: Pre-register the OOS rules and PASS/WATCH/FAIL gates for Crypto Daily Crash-Candle Reversion v1 BEFORE any 2024-2025 bar is read. Crypto-D11 returned IS_WATCH (not IS_CONTINUE): the IS profile is strong on BTC primary (69 trades, net expectancy +0.95%/trade, top-3 removal positive on all symbols) but the single-year-dominance hard gate FAILED (BTC 2020 = 96% of net, combined 2021 = 98%) and every symbol lost in the 2022 bear. This protocol exists so the OOS test is judged against rules fixed in advance, and so the OOS explicitly measures whether any edge is regime-robust or just another single-period artifact.

2_frozen_strategy_reference.no_rule_changes_allowed: The OOS run MUST use the exact frozen CCR1 v1 rules with zero parameter freedom. No threshold change, no added filter, no exit change, no variant. Any change is a NEW spec id and a fresh ladder, never an edit here.

3_oos_window.data: frozen snapshot data_crypto/spot_binance_usdt_1d_2020_2025/ (D3b 5b3d94c) - the same immutable BTC/ETH/SOL spot daily USDT files, OOS slice only
3_oos_window.warmup_note: v1 has NO trend/SMA filter, so OOS needs no pre-2024 warmup; slice each symbol to 2024-2025 before any computation. A late-2025 entry that cannot complete its 3-bar horizon by 2025-12-31 exits data_end at the 2025-12-31 close - no 2026 bar is read.
3_oos_window.boundary_seal: No 2026 row may enter the engine. rows_after_2025_used MUST be 0.

4_oos_run_rules.exit: close of the 3rd bar after entry (entry bar counts as bar 1; exit_index = entry_index + 2)
4_oos_run_rules.no_reruns_or_variants: the OOS is run ONCE per symbol; no second look, no parameter sweep, no alternative threshold, no cherry-picked sub-window

metrics: year-by-year 2024 vs 2025 (trade count + net base per year)
single-year dominance (max OOS-year share of combined net)
BTC primary standalone performance (must carry its own evidence)
5_required_oos_metrics.data_qa: rows, date range, duplicate timestamps, invalid OHLC, zero/neg volume, rows_after_2025_used (must be 0) per symbol

FAIL_if_any: only one symbol positive after friction
one OOS year contributes >80% of combined net AND the other year is <= 0
rule mismatch (any deviation from the frozen CCR1 v1 rules)
WATCH_if: 2/3 symbols positive but one major symbol weak
one year dominates 60-80% of combined net
trade count low but not fail
PASS_only_if_all: top-3 removal positive for BTC AND combined
at least 2/3 symbols positive after friction
6_gates.precedence: FAIL triggers are checked FIRST and are absolute - any single FAIL trigger forces FAIL regardless of other metrics. PASS requires ALL PASS conditions simultaneously. Anything that is neither a clean PASS nor a FAIL is WATCH. ETH/SOL strength may NOT rescue a BTC-primary FAIL.

7_interpretation.pass_is_not_paper_ready: A PASS verdict means the IS edge survived a single pre-registered OOS test. It does NOT mean the strategy is paper-ready or live-ready; later ladder steps (significance, full sequence risk, regime, multi-asset, walk-forward, friction, final decision) remain.
7_interpretation.watch_means_continue_validation_only: WATCH means the family may continue down the validation ladder under separate authorization - not deploy.
7_interpretation.fail_means_park: FAIL means park crash-candle v1; NO OOS rerun, NO second look, NO parameter tuning.
7_interpretation.dominance_fail_no_tuning: If OOS fails specifically from single-year / single-period dominance, do NOT tune parameters or add filters to chase robustness - that would be overfitting to the OOS. Park the family or open a NEW pre-registered spec id.
7_interpretation.regime_robustness_first_class: Per the D11 weakness, single-year/regime dominance is a FIRST-CLASS OOS acceptance criterion here (gates: >80% single-year-with-other-year<=0 => FAIL; 60-80% => WATCH; >60% blocks PASS).

9_final_line: Crypto-D12 is an OOS protocol only; no OOS run, crypto backtest, exchange API, paper trading, or live trading is authorized.
